title: ActivityExecutor actor core implementation
description: Implement ActivityExecutor actor in wtf-actor/src/activity.rs based on ractor. Core struct with activity_name field. Implement pre_start initialization and handle method for message processing. Follow ractor actor patterns from ADR-003.

title: ActivityState with status tracking and retry counting
description: Define ActivityState struct with status (ActivityStatus enum: Pending, Running, Completed, Failed, Cancelled) and retry_count (u32). Implement state transitions and persistence for journal replay.

title: ActivityMsg message types and RpcReplyPort handling
description: Implement ActivityMsg enum: Execute { input: Vec<u8>, reply: RpcReplyPort<Result<Vec<u8>, ActivityError>> }, GetStatus(RpcReplyPort<ActivityStatus>), Cancel { reply: RpcReplyPort<()> }. Use ractor's Message derive macro.

title: ActivityError enum with Retryable, Fatal, Timeout variants
description: Define ActivityError enum with Retryable(String), Fatal(String), Timeout variants. Implement std::error::Error and From traits for conversion from activity handler errors.

title: Retry logic with exponential backoff
description: Implement retry logic in ActivityExecutor.handle(). On Retryable error, increment retry_count and schedule re-execution with exponential backoff (base 2, max 5 retries, max_delay 60s). Use tokio::time::sleep for async delay. Max retries configurable via ActivityConfig.

title: Timeout handling for activity execution
description: Implement timeout handling using ractor's call_t! pattern or tokio::time::timeout. Default timeout configurable via ActivityConfig. On timeout, return ActivityError::Timeout and do not retry (terminal state).

title: ActivityRegistry with register_activity and get_activity
description: Implement ActivityRegistry as a global registry (Arc<RwLock<HashMap>>). Provide register_activity(name: String, handler_fn: ActivityHandler) and get_activity(name: &str) -> Option<ActivityHandler>. ActivityHandler is a boxed async function: Fn(Vec<u8>) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ActivityError>.

title: WorkflowInstance spawns ActivityActor
description: Modify WorkflowInstance (wtf-actor/src/instance.rs) to spawn ActivityActor via Actor::spawn_linked when executing activity steps. Pass activity_name and config. Link supervision so ActivityActor failure propagates to WorkflowInstance.

title: Cancel operation with graceful shutdown
description: Implement Cancel message handling. Set ActivityState.status = Cancelled. If activity is running, send cancellation signal to handler (via channel). Reply to RpcReplyPort once cancellation confirmed or after graceful timeout.

title: Integration tests for activity execution
description: Write integration tests: 1) successful activity execution, 2) retry on Retryable error, 3) no retry on Fatal error, 4) timeout after configured duration, 5) cancel during execution, 6) registry lookup and invocation. Use tempfile for temp storage.

title: Define core data structures and error types
description: Create ActivityHandler, ActivityRegistry structs, and ActivityError enum with variants for NotFound, Timeout, ExecutionFailed, and RegistrationError. Implement Default for trivial defaults.

title: Implement registration methods
description: Implement register(name, version, handler, description), get(name) -> Option<ActivityHandler>, list() -> Vec<ActivityHandler>, unregister(name) -> Result<(), ActivityError>. Use name:version format for HashMap key.

title: Implement activity execution with timeout
description: Implement execute(activity_name, input, timeout_duration) -> Result<Vec<u8>, ActivityError>. Wrap handler execution in tokio::time::timeout. Return Timeout error if exceeded.

title: Implement retry with exponential backoff
description: Add retry configuration to ActivityRegistry: max_retries (default 3), base_delay (default 100ms). Implement exponential backoff: delay = base_delay * 2^attempt. Log retry attempts.

title: Implement built-in activities
description: Implement sleep(duration_ms) and wait_for_signal(name) as built-in activities registered on ActivityRegistry initialization. sleep uses tokio::time::sleep. wait_for_signal uses a channel-based signal mechanism.

title: Integration tests for activity registry
description: Comprehensive integration tests covering: register/get/unregister lifecycle, concurrent access, timeout enforcement, retry behavior, built-in sleep accuracy, signal send/receive.

title: Create client module with Client struct and constructors
description:
Implement wtf-cli/src/client.rs with:
- Client struct: base_url (String), http (reqwest::Client)
- Client::new(base_url: &str) -> Self: Creates client with default reqwest::Client
- Client::new_with_timeout(base_url: &str, timeout: Duration) -> Self: Creates client with custom timeout
- CliError enum: NetworkError, ApiError { code, message }, NotFound, InvalidResponse, Timeout
- impl std::fmt::Display for CliError with user-friendly messages
- impl std::error::Error for CliError
- From<reqwest::Error> for CliError conversion

Follow functional-rust: Data types first, then impl blocks.

title: Implement API types for client requests/responses
Implement start_workflow client method
description:
impl Client method:
async fn start_workflow(&self, name: &str, input: &str) -> Result<String>

- Builds POST /api/v1/workflows request
- Serializes input as JSON
- Returns invocation_id on success
- Maps reqwest::Error -> CliError
- Returns CliError::InvalidResponse on parse failure
- Handles API error responses with user-friendly message

title: Implement get_status client method
description:
impl Client method:
async fn get_status(&self, id: &str) -> Result<WorkflowStatus>

- Builds GET /api/v1/workflows/{id} request
- Parses WorkflowStatus from JSON response
- Returns CliError::NotFound if API returns 404
- Maps reqwest::Error -> CliError
- Returns user-friendly error on API error response

title: Implement list_workflows client method
description:
impl Client method:
async fn list_workflows(&self, status: Option<&str>) -> Result<Vec<WorkflowInfo>>

- Builds GET /api/v1/workflows request
- Appends ?status=<status> query param if provided
- Parses Vec<WorkflowInfo> from JSON response
- Maps reqwest::Error -> CliError
- Handles empty list as valid response

title: Implement send_signal client method
description:
impl Client method:
async fn send_signal(&self, id: &str, name: &str, payload: &str) -> Result<()>

- Builds POST /api/v1/workflows/{id}/signals request
- Serializes SignalRequest { signal_name: name, payload: parsed json }
- Returns Ok(()) on 200 OK with acknowledged: true
- Returns CliError::NotFound if workflow not found
- Maps reqwest::Error -> CliError
- Returns CliError::InvalidResponse if payload is not valid JSON

title: Implement get_journal client method
description:
impl Client method:
async fn get_journal(&self, id: &str) -> Result<JournalResponse>

- Builds GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}/journal request
- Parses JournalResponse from JSON
- Returns CliError::NotFound if workflow not found
- Maps reqwest::Error -> CliError
- Handles empty entries vec as valid response

title: Implement terminate client method
description:
impl Client method:
async fn terminate(&self, id: &str) -> Result<()>

- Builds DELETE /api/v1/workflows/{id} request
- Returns Ok(()) on 204 No Content
- Returns CliError::NotFound if workflow not found
- Maps reqwest::Error -> CliError
- Returns user-friendly error for other API errors

title: Add Client integration tests
Create API types module with request/response structs
description: Define StartWorkflowRequest, StartWorkflowResponse, WorkflowStatus, SignalRequest, SignalResponse, JournalResponse, ApiError with Json schemas and From implementations for error conversion. Follow functional-rust Data→Calc→Actions: this is pure Data definitions.

title: Implement start_workflow handler
description: POST /api/v1/workflows - Validates StartWorkflowRequest, calls workflow service, returns StartWorkflowResponse with invocation_id. Uses Json extractor, returns StatusCode on error.

title: Implement get_workflow handler
description: GET /api/v1/workflows/:id - Extracts invocation_id from Path, queries workflow service, returns WorkflowStatus. Returns 404 if not found.

title: Implement delete_workflow handler
description: DELETE /api/v1/workflows/:id - Terminates workflow by invocation_id. Returns 204 on success, 404 if not found.

title: Implement send_signal handler
description: POST /api/v1/workflows/:id/signals - Extracts invocation_id and SignalRequest payload, sends signal to workflow, returns SignalResponse.

title: Implement get_journal handler
description: GET /api/v1/workflows/:id/journal - Returns JournalResponse with workflow execution journal/events. Returns 404 if workflow not found.

title: Implement list_workflows handler
description: GET /api/v1/workflows - Returns list of all workflows with pagination support via query params. Returns Json<Vec<WorkflowStatus>>.

title: Implement health_check handler
description: GET /health - Returns 200 OK with health status JSON. Simple liveness probe for load balancers.

title: Wire up Router with all endpoints
description: Create Router in lib.rs combining all handlers: /api/v1/workflows, /api/v1/workflows/:id, /api/v1/workflows/:id/signals, /api/v1/workflows/:id/journal, /health. Apply correct HTTP methods and nesting.

title: Create CLI request/response types module
description: Define CliRequest, CliResponse, WorkflowListItem, JournalEntry types that mirror the API types but are CLI-specific. Include Display impl for JSON output formatting. This is pure Data per functional-rust pattern.

title: Implement ApiClient HTTP client
description: Create ApiClient struct with reqwest::Client for HTTP calls to the API. Implement methods: start_workflow, get_status, list_workflows, send_signal, get_journal, terminate_workflow. Use functional-rust: Data→Calc→Actions with Result return types.

title: Implement Commands enum with Clap derive
description: Create the Commands enum with clap::Parser derive as specified: Start { name, input }, Status { id }, List { status }, Signal { id, name, payload }, Journal { id }, Terminate { id }, Serve { port }. Each variant has the correct arg attributes.

title: Implement run_start command handler
description: Handler for Start command: validates input is valid JSON, calls client.start_workflow(), prints JSON response with invocation_id, workflow_name, status, started_at. Handles errors with JSON error output.

title: Implement run_status command handler
description: Handler for Status command: calls client.get_status(id), prints JSON response with invocation_id, workflow_name, status, current_step, started_at, updated_at. Returns exit code 1 on not found.

title: Implement run_list command handler
description: Handler for List command: calls client.list_workflows(status_filter), prints JSON array of WorkflowListItem. Filters by status if provided. Empty array if no matches.

title: Implement run_signal command handler
description: Handler for Signal command: validates payload is valid JSON, calls client.send_signal(id, name, payload), prints JSON response with acknowledged: true. Handles not found errors.

title: Implement run_journal command handler
description: Handler for Journal command: calls client.get_journal(id), prints JSON response with invocation_id and entries array. Each entry has seq, type, name, input, output, timestamp.

title: Implement run_terminate command handler
description: Handler for Terminate command: calls client.terminate_workflow(id), prints empty JSON {} on success. Returns exit code 1 on not found.

title: Implement run_serve command handler
description: Handler for Serve command: starts the API server by calling into wtf-api. Uses port from args (default 8080). Initializes sled, sets up router, starts Axum server. Prints startup info to stdout.

title: Implement main function with command dispatch
description: Main entry point: parse Commands with clap, match variants and call appropriate run_* handler. Initialize tracing for logging. Use exit(exit_code) for proper exit codes. All JSON output via serde_json::to_writer_pretty.

title: Add CLI error types module
description: Implement CliError enum: ApiError(ApiError), JsonParseError(serde_json::Error), NetworkError(reqwest::Error), NotFound(String), InvalidInput(String). Implement thiserror::Error, Display, From conversions.

title: Write CLI integration tests
description: Write tests for command handlers using a mock API server. Test JSON serialization of requests/responses. Test error handling paths. Test input validation for Start and Signal commands.

title: bead: InvocationId ULID wrapper type
description:
Create InvocationId newtype wrapper around ulid::Ulid with:
- ULID generation (ulid::Ulid::new())
- Serialization to/from ULID string format
- Display trait impl
- FromStr parsing
- Eq/Ord for use in HashMap/BTreeMap
- DEFAULT constant for placeholder values
Acceptance: Invocations can be created, serialized, and compared

title: bead: WorkflowName type with validation
description:
Create WorkflowName newtype wrapping String with:
- Validation (non-empty, max 256 chars, alphanumeric + underscore + hyphen)
- Display trait impl
- FromStr parsing with validation
- Serialization to/from JSON
- Error type: WorkflowNameError
Acceptance: Invalid workflow names are rejected at creation time

title: bead: InstanceStatus enum
description:
Implement InstanceStatus enum per ADR-007:
- Running (workflow actively executing)
- Completed (workflow finished successfully)
- Failed(String) (workflow failed with reason)
- Suspended (workflow paused waiting for signal)
- Serialize/Deserialize with JSON
- Display trait for logging
Acceptance: Status correctly represents workflow lifecycle states

title: bead: DurationSecs wrapper type
description:
Create DurationSecs wrapper around u64 for durations:
- Max 24 hours constraint (86400 seconds) per ADR-004
- TryFrom<u64> for validated construction
- Serialization to/from JSON (as seconds integer)
- Error type: DurationExceeds24Hours
- Conversion to std::time::Duration
Acceptance: Durations > 24h are rejected

title: bead: JournalEntry enum
bead: RetryPolicy and CatchPolicy types
description:
Implement error handling policies per ADR-004:

RetryPolicy:
- errors: Vec<String> (error names to retry)
- interval_sec: u64
- max_attempts: u32
- backoff_rate: f64

CatchPolicy:
- errors: Vec<String>
- next: String (state to transition to)
- result_path: Option<String>

Both with Serialize/Deserialize
Acceptance: Retry and catch behaviors are configurable per state

title: bead: StateConfig enum (Step Functions parity)
bead: ChoiceRule type for branching
description:
Implement ChoiceRule for Choice state:
- String comparisons: StringEquals, StringLessThan, StringGreaterThan, etc.
- Numeric comparisons: NumericEquals, NumericLessThan, etc.
- Boolean and timestamp comparisons
- And/Or combinators for complex conditions
- Serialize/Deserialize with JSON
Acceptance: All Step Functions comparison operators supported

title: bead: StateNode and Connection types for DAG
description:
Implement DAG node types per ADR-002:

StateNode:
- id: u32
- name: String
- state_type: String (for serde tagging)
- config: StateConfig

Connection:
- source_port: String (main, true, false, branch_N)
- target_port: String

Serialize/Deserialize with JSON
Acceptance: Workflow DAG structure is serializable

title: bead: InstanceData struct for storage
description:
Implement InstanceData per ADR-007:
- name: WorkflowName
- status: InstanceStatus
- input: Vec<u8>
- output: Option<Vec<u8>>
- current_step: u32
- created_at: i64 (Unix timestamp)
- updated_at: i64

Serialize/Deserialize with JSON
Acceptance: Instance metadata can be persisted to sled

title: bead: TimerData and SignalData types
description:
Implement per ADR-007:

TimerData:
- invocation_id: InvocationId
- seq: u32
- fire_at: i64

SignalData:
- invocation_id: InvocationId
- name: String
- payload: Vec<u8>
- received_at: i64

Serialize/Deserialize with JSON
Acceptance: Timers and signals can be stored and retrieved

title: bead: WfError main error enum
bead: StartError, SignalError, StepError types
description:
Implement error types per ADR-010:

StartError:
- WorkflowNotFound(String)
- InvalidInput(String)
- AtCapacity { running, max }

SignalError:
- InvocationNotFound(String)
- SignalNotAllowed { signal, state }

StepError:
- Retryable(String)
- Fatal(String)
- Timeout(String)
- Cancelled

thiserror derive, Serialize, Display
Acceptance: Errors are actionable and serializable

title: bead: ApiError for HTTP responses
description:
Implement ApiError per ADR-010:
- error: String (machine-readable code)
- message: String (human-readable)
- details: Option<serde_json::Value>
- retry_after_seconds: Option<u64>

From<&WfError> conversion implementation
Serialize for JSON responses
Acceptance: API errors serialize correctly for HTTP responses

title: bead: Serialization module with JSON/MessagePack/YAML
description:
Create serialization helpers module:
- JsonSerializer: serialize_to_json, deserialize_from_json
- MsgPackSerializer: serialize_to_msgpack, deserialize_from_msgpack (using rmp-serde)
- YamlSerializer: serialize_to_yaml, deserialize_from_yaml
- AnySerializer enum for runtime selection
- Result type alias: SerResult<T> = Result<T, SerError>

Acceptance: Workflows can be serialized to multiple formats

title: bead: JournalEntry_serde tests
description:
Write roundtrip tests for JournalEntry:
- Each variant serializes to JSON and deserializes back
- Verify no data loss in roundtrip
- Test with various input sizes
Acceptance: Journal entries survive JSON serialization roundtrips

title: bead: StateConfig_serde tests
description:
Write roundtrip tests for StateConfig:
- Each state type serializes correctly with Type tag
- Deserialize from JSON matches original
- Verify retry/catch policies serialize correctly
Acceptance: State configs survive JSON serialization roundtrips

title: bead: InvocationId Ulid generation tests
description:
Write tests for InvocationId:
- Generated IDs are unique
- IDs sort correctly (Lexical Ultralite)
- Serialization to string and back works
- Display formatting correct
Acceptance: ULID generation is correct and unique

title: Define JournalEntry types per ADR-005
description: Create JournalEntry enum with Run, Sleep, WaitForSignal, GetState, SetState, Choice, Parallel, Complete, Error variants. Implement JournalEntry::from_output and entry lookup methods.

title: Define RunFuture and DurableFuture types
description: Create RunFuture<T> wrapping future with journal replay capability. Create DurableFuture<O> for state operations. Both must support journal integration and suspension/resumption.

title: Define DurableContext trait
description: Define the DurableContext trait with run, get_state, set_state, sleep, wait_for_signal, signal, rand_uuid methods. Include documentation per ADR-004 state types.

title: Implement WorkflowContext struct with journal replay
description: Implement WorkflowContext that holds journal reference, instance ID, and implements DurableContext. Integrates journal replay per ADR-005 algorithm: check journal for cached result, execute if not found, journal the result.

title: Implement state management (get_state/set_state)
description: Implement get_state and set_state with journal integration. get_state checks journal entry for cached value. set_state journals the state change. Uses MessagePack serialization.

title: Implement signaling (wait_for_signal/signal)
description: Implement wait_for_signal and signal. Signals are named channels with payload. wait_for_signal suspends until signal arrives. Uses in-memory signal registry with journal backup.

title: Implement sleep with journal replay support
description: Implement sleep(duration) that suspends until duration passes. On replay, if sleep was already completed (fire_at < now), return immediately. Journal Sleep entries with fire_at timestamp.

title: Implement rand_uuid for WorkflowContext
description: Implement rand_uuid() using uuid::Uuid::new_v4. Must be deterministic for replay - journal the UUID so replay returns same value. This is critical for idempotency keys.

title: Create context module and export types
description: Create wtf-core/src/context.rs that exports DurableContext, RunFuture, DurableFuture, WorkflowContext. Update lib.rs to re-export context types.

title: dag: Define StateType enum and StateNode struct
description: Implement core StateType enum (Pass, Task, Wait, Choice, Parallel, Map, Succeed, Fail) and StateNode struct per ADR-002

title: dag: Define Connection struct and WorkflowGraph type alias
description: Implement Connection struct with source_port/target_port and WorkflowGraph = DiGraph<StateNode, Connection> type alias

title: dag: Implement WorkflowDag wrapper with execution_order and ready_nodes
description: Implement WorkflowDag wrapper struct with entry_point, and methods: execution_order() using toposort, ready_nodes() for completed predecessor detection

title: dag: Implement edges_from and parallel_branches methods
description: Implement edges_from() for port-filtered edge traversal and parallel_branches() for detecting fan-out from Parallel state nodes

title: dag: Add serde serialization tests and integration
description: Add comprehensive serialization tests for WorkflowDAG, verify JSON and MessagePack roundtrip, ensure serde attributes are correct

title: errors: Implement WfError enum with all variants
errors: Implement ApiError and From trait implementations
Create workflow_node.rs with Step Functions WorkflowNode enum
description: Implement WorkflowNode enum with full Step Functions parity: Pass, Task, Wait, Choice, Parallel, Map, Succeed, Fail. Each variant holds its config type with all required fields per ADR-004. Include serde derives for serialization.

title: Implement Config types (PassConfig, TaskConfig, WaitConfig, ChoiceConfig, ParallelConfig, MapConfig, SucceedConfig, FailConfig)
description: Create all Step Functions config types with exact field parity from ADR-004: PassConfig (result, result_path), TaskConfig (resource, timeout, heartbeat, retry, catch), WaitConfig (seconds max 86400, timestamp), ChoiceConfig (choice: Vec<ChoiceRule>, default), ParallelConfig (branches, retry, catch), MapConfig (iterator, items_path, max_concurrency), SucceedConfig (output), FailConfig (error, cause). Include RetryPolicy and CatchPolicy.

title: Implement Connection types (PortName, Connection)
description: Create PortName type alias for connection port identifiers (main, true, false, branch_0, etc.) and Connection struct with source, target, source_port, target_port fields. These form the edge types for the DAG.

title: Implement ExecutionState state machine and NodeExecutionStatus tracking
description: Create ExecutionState enum (Idle, Queued, Running, Completed, Failed, Skipped) and NodeExecutionStatus struct for tracking individual node execution status. Include methods for state transitions and status updates.

title: Create dag.rs with petgraph integration for workflow DAG
description: Implement DAG structure using petgraph for workflow representation. Include NodeId mapping, edge management, topological traversal, and cycle detection. Integrate with WorkflowNode and Connection types.

title: Implement validation.rs for workflow validation
description: Create validation module for workflow graphs: validate no cycles, all referenced nodes exist, wait times <= 86400 seconds, choice rules have valid operators, parallel/map branches are properly nested, terminal states (Succeed/Fail) have no outgoing edges.

title: Add ChoiceRule and error handling types (RetryPolicy, CatchPolicy)
description: Implement ChoiceRule with all comparison operators (StringEquals, NumericEquals, etc.) and the error handling types RetryPolicy (errors, interval_sec, max_attempts, backoff_rate) and CatchPolicy (errors, next, result_path).

title: Create lib.rs exports for graph module
description: Export all graph module types from lib.rs. Include WorkflowNode, all Config types, Connection types, ExecutionState, and validation functions. Ensure clean public API surface.

title: Adapt wtf-frontend lib.rs module structure for Oya fork
description: Set up module structure: pub mod ui, pub mod graph, pub mod wtf_client. Create ui/mod.rs with canvas, toolbar, sidebar, inspector, history_panel, journal_viewer exports. Create graph/ and wtf_client/ stub modules per ADR-011 module structure.

title: Create wtf_client/client.rs with HTTP client for wtf-api
description: Implement WtfClient struct with base_url and reqwest::Client. Methods: get_journal(invocation_id) -> Vec<JournalEntry>, start_workflow(name, input) -> invocation_id, get_workflow(name) -> Workflow, list_workflows() -> Vec<WorkflowSummary>, get_execution_state(invocation_id) -> ExecutionState. Replace Restate SDK calls with HTTP API calls per ADR-011.

title: Create wtf_client/types.rs with API types
description: Define JournalEntry { entry_index, entry_type, timestamp, payload }, JournalResponse { entries: Vec<JournalEntry> }, StartRequest { workflow_name, input }, StartResponse { invocation_id }, Workflow { name, definition, created_at }, WorkflowSummary { name, last_run }. These map to the wtf-api HTTP endpoints.

title: Create ui/toolbar.rs adapted from Oya FlowToolbar
description: Adapt Oya FlowToolbar: Replace workflow_name signal with wtf_client call. Replace on_execute to call start_workflow API. Keep all UI elements (zoom, layout, undo/redo, save, settings). Update state badges: running=blue, completed=green, failed=red, waiting=yellow. Workflow name becomes editable input that saves to wtf-api.

title: Create ui/sidebar.rs NodeSidebar with Step Functions node palette
description: Adapt Oya NodeSidebar/PrototypePalette: Replace Restate node types (HttpHandler, KafkaHandler, etc.) with Step Functions nodes: Pass, Task, Wait, Choice, Parallel, Map, Succeed, Fail. Add HttpTrigger, CronTrigger for workflow entry points. Categories: Flow Control, Execution, Triggers. Node templates grouped by category per ADR-004.

title: Create ui/inspector.rs InspectorPanel for node configuration
description: Adapt Oya InspectorPanel/SelectedNodePanel: Display Step Functions node config (Task.resource, Wait.seconds, Choice.rules, etc.). Fields change based on node type. Include retry/catch editor for Task nodes. Output section shows node execution result. Status badge: running=blue, completed=green, failed=red, waiting=yellow.

title: Create ui/canvas.rs Canvas workflow editor component
description: Adapt Oya canvas (node rendering, edge rendering, viewport, drag-drop): Connect to wtf_client for workflow definition. Use WorkflowNode types instead of Restate nodes. Display execution state on each node with color-coded borders. Pan/zoom via mouse. Inline config panel for quick edits. Selection and multi-select support.

title: Create ui/history_panel.rs ExecutionHistoryPanel
description: Adapt Oya ExecutionHistoryPanel: Display list of workflow runs from wtf_client.list_workflows(). Show invocation_id, status (success/failure), timestamp, duration. Click to view execution details. Status badges: completed=green, failed=red. Replace Restate invocation records with wtf-engine run records.

title: Create ui/journal_viewer.rs JournalViewer component (NEW)
description: Implement new JournalViewer per ADR-011: Fetch journal entries via wtf_client.get_journal(invocation_id). Display as vertical timeline with entry cards. Each entry shows: index, type (Start, Exit, StateEntered, ActivityScheduled, etc.), timestamp, payload preview. Click entry to expand full payload in side panel. Timeline connector lines between entries.

title: Create ui/state_badges.rs with Step Functions state colors
description: Create reusable status badge components. Colors per ADR-011: running=blue (bg-blue-100 text-blue-700), completed=green (bg-green-100 text-green-700), failed=red (bg-red-100 text-red-700), waiting=yellow (bg-amber-100 text-amber-700). Functions: status_badge_class(state), status_badge_label(state), execution_state_label(state).

title: Integrate all UI components into app shell
description: Create main app layout composing: Toolbar (top), Sidebar (left node palette), Canvas (center), Inspector (right when node selected), HistoryPanel (bottom drawer), JournalViewer (modal/panel on invocation click). Wire up wtf_client for all data. Ensure responsive layout with CSS grid/flexbox.

title: Add Dioxus router and WASM build configuration
description: Set up dioxus-router for /, /workflow/:name, /workflow/:name/run/:invocation_id routes. Configure Cargo.toml with wasm32-unknown-unknown target. Add web build script. Ensure reqwest uses rustls-tls for WASM compatibility. Test build with cargo build --target wasm32-unknown-unknown.

title: bead: MasterOrchestrator struct and OrchestratorState
description:
Define MasterOrchestrator and OrchestratorState per ADR-006:

MasterOrchestrator:
- max_concurrent: usize (capacity limit)
- storage: Arc<sled::Db> (journal persistence)

OrchestratorState:
- instances: HashMap<String, (String, ActorRef<InstanceMsg>)> mapping invocation_id -> (workflow_name, actor_ref)
- running_count: usize

Impl Actor for MasterOrchestrator with pre_start initialization.
Acceptance: Structs compile, state initializes empty registry and 0 running count.

title: bead: OrchestratorMsg enum with all variants
bead: capacity_check method
description:
Implement capacity_check method:

fn capacity_check(&self, state: &OrchestratorState) -> bool {
    state.running_count < self.max_concurrent
}

Returns true if below capacity, false if at limit.
Used before spawning new workflow instances.
Acceptance: Returns correct boolean for capacity comparison.

title: bead: spawn_workflow method
description:
Implement spawn_workflow helper:

async fn spawn_workflow(
    &self,
    myself: ActorRef<Self::Msg>,
    state: &mut OrchestratorState,
    name: String,
    invocation_id: String,
    input: Vec<u8>,
) -> Result<ActorRef<InstanceMsg>, ActorProcessingErr>

Uses Actor::spawn_linked with:
- Name: format!("{}:{}", name, invocation_id)
- Actor: WorkflowInstance::new(name.clone())
- Config: InstanceConfig { invocation_id, input, storage: self.storage.clone() }
- Supervisor: myself.clone().into()

Inserts into state.instances after spawn.
Acceptance: WorkflowInstance actor spawns linked under MasterOrchestrator supervision.

title: bead: handle StartWorkflow message
description:
Implement StartWorkflow handling in handle():

1. Check capacity with capacity_check()
2. If at capacity, reply.send(Err(StartError::AtCapacity))
3. Generate invocation_id (ULID-based)
4. Call spawn_workflow to create actor
5. Increment state.running_count
6. Reply with invocation_id

Return Ok(()) after handling.
Acceptance: Workflow starts only when under capacity, returns invocation_id.

title: bead: handle GetStatus message
description:
Implement GetStatus handling in handle():

1. Lookup invocation_id in state.instances
2. If not found, reply.send(None)
3. If found, cast GetStatus to workflow actor and reply with result
4. Handle timeout or actor communication errors gracefully

Reply: RpcReplyPort<Option<InstanceStatus>>
Acceptance: Returns status for running workflows, None for unknown.

title: bead: handle Signal message
description:
Implement Signal handling in handle():

1. Lookup invocation_id in state.instances
2. If not found, reply.send(Err(SignalError::InvocationNotFound))
3. If found, cast Signal { signal_name, payload } to workflow actor
4. Propagate reply from workflow actor

Reply: RpcReplyPort<Result<(), SignalError>>
Acceptance: Signals route to correct workflow actor or return error.

title: bead: handle ListWorkflows message
description:
Implement ListWorkflows handling in handle():

1. Iterate state.instances HashMap
2. For each entry, query workflow actor for current status via cast
3. Collect WorkflowInfo { invocation_id, name, status, started_at }
4. Reply with Vec<WorkflowInfo>

Reply: RpcReplyPort<Vec<WorkflowInfo>>
Acceptance: Returns list of all running workflows with current status.

title: bead: handle Terminate message
description:
Implement Terminate handling in handle():

1. Lookup invocation_id in state.instances
2. If not found, reply.send(Err(TerminateError::InvocationNotFound))
3. If found, send Complete or Fail message to workflow actor to stop it
4. Remove from state.instances
5. Decrement state.running_count
6. Reply with Ok(())

Reply: RpcReplyPort<Result<(), TerminateError>>
Acceptance: Workflow terminates cleanly and is removed from registry.

title: bead: handle_supervisor_evt for child lifecycle
description:
Implement handle_supervisor_evt per ADR-006:

Match SupervisionEvent variants:
- ActorTerminated(cell, _, reason): Remove from instances, decrement running_count
- ActorFailed(cell, error): Remove from instances, decrement running_count, log error
- ActorStarted(cell): Increment running_count, log info
- _ => {} (ignore others)

Use state.instances.retain() to filter out terminated actors.
Acceptance: Child lifecycle events correctly update registry and counter.

title: bead: MasterOrchestrator actor builder and tests
description:
Implement Actor::spawn for MasterOrchestrator:

1. Create Actor::spawn_linked with pre_start initialization
2. Verify state starts with empty instances HashMap
3. Test capacity_check returns true when under limit
4. Test capacity_check returns false when at limit
5. Test spawn_workflow creates actor and inserts into registry
6. Test handle_supervisor_evt cleans up terminated actors
7. Test max_concurrent enforcement blocks new workflows when at capacity

Use mocked storage for testing.
Acceptance: MasterOrchestrator correctly manages workflow lifecycle.

title: bead: Implement error mapping from OrchestratorMsg errors to HTTP status codes
description:
Implement error mapping per ADR-010:
- StartError::WorkflowNotFound -> 404
- StartError::InvalidInput -> 400
- StartError::AtCapacity -> 409 Conflict with retry_after_seconds
- SignalError::InvocationNotFound -> 404
- SignalError::SignalNotAllowed -> 422
- General errors -> 500

Return ApiError struct with error, message, retry_after_seconds fields.

title: bead: Implement create_routes function with all middleware layers
description:
Implement create_routes function per ADR-012:
```rust
pub fn create_routes(master: ActorRef<OrchestratorMsg>) -> Router {
    Router::new()
        .route("/health", get(health))
        .route("/api/v1/workflows", post(start_workflow).get(list_workflows))
        .route("/api/v1/workflows/:id", get(get_workflow).delete(terminate_workflow))
        .route("/api/v1/workflows/:id/signals", post(send_signal))
        .route("/api/v1/workflows/:id/journal", get(get_journal))
        .layer(TraceLayer::new())
        .layer(Extension(master))
        .layer(JsonBodyLayer::<JsonBody>::new())
}
```

title: bead: Add OpenAPI doc comments to handlers
bead: Export types and handlers from wtf-api lib
sled-storage/db: Define Database struct and tree constants
description: Define tree name constants and Database struct wrapping sled::Db with tree handle fields for all 7 trees (instances, journal, timers, signals, run_queue, activities, workflows).

title: sled-storage/db: Implement open_db function
description: Implement open_db(path) that opens or creates a sled database at the given path and opens all 7 trees (instances, journal, timers, signals, run_queue, activities, workflows). Returns Database wrapping the opened sled::Db.

title: sled-storage/db: Implement close_db function
description: Implement close_db() that performs graceful shutdown of the database. Calls flush before closing to ensure all data is persisted.

title: sled-storage/db: Implement get_tree function
description: Implement get_tree(name) that retrieves a tree by name. Returns a cloned tree handle for the given tree name. Returns Err(DbError) if tree name is unknown.

title: sled-storage/db: Implement flush_all function
description: Implement flush_all() that flushes all 7 trees to disk using flush_async. Returns Result<(), DbError>.

title: sled-storage/db: Add integration tests
description: Add comprehensive integration tests for the db module covering open_db, close_db, get_tree, and flush_all with temp directory handling.

title: sled-storage/journal: Define JournalEntry types and key encoding
description: Define JournalEntry enum per ADR-005 and key encoding helpers for journal tree. JournalEntry variants: Run, Sleep, WaitForSignal, GetState, SetState, Choice, Parallel, Complete, Error. Key format: invocation_id:seq.

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement append_journal_entry function
description: Implement append_journal_entry that inserts a journal entry into the JOURNAL tree with key invocation_id:seq and calls flush_async after insert.

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement get_journal_entry function
description: Implement get_journal_entry that retrieves a single journal entry by invocation_id and seq. Returns Option<JournalEntry>.

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement get_all_journal_entries function
description: Implement get_all_journal_entries that retrieves all journal entries for an invocation using prefix scan. Returns Vec<(seq, JournalEntry)> sorted by seq ascending.

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement get_journal_length function
description: Implement get_journal_length that returns the count of journal entries for an invocation. Returns u32.

title: sled-storage/journal: Implement delete_journal function
description: Implement delete_journal that removes all journal entries for an invocation. Uses scan_prefix to find all entries then removes them.

title: sled-storage/journal: Add comprehensive unit tests
description: Add comprehensive unit tests for all journal functions using temp directory for sled database isolation.

title: signals: Define SignalData struct and key encoding per ADR-007
signals: Implement store_signal and get_signal functions

wtf-actor: Define SupervisionEvent enum and supervisor message handler
wtf-actor: Define RestartPolicy enum and SupervisorConfig struct
wtf-actor: Implement restart_child() with retry tracking and delay
wtf-actor: Implement stop_all_children() and get_children() supervisor methods
wtf-actor: Implement escalation handling and death notification to parent
wtf-worker: Define Worker struct with storage, master, registry
description: Define the Worker struct with Arc<sled::Db>, ActorRef<OrchestratorMsg>, and ActivityRegistry. Implements Send + Sync for thread-safety.

title: wtf-worker: Implement dequeue_workflow from run_queue
description: Poll the run_queue in storage for next invocation_id. Uses tokio::sync::mpsc channel or sled市民trees. Returns Option<InvocationId>

title: wtf-worker: Implement check_expired_timers poll mechanism
description: Poll the timers tree for expired timers. Each timer has a deadline. Returns Option<Timer> for next expired timer.

title: wtf-worker: Implement check_pending_signals poll mechanism
description: Poll signals tree for pending signals destined for workflows. Signals include: cancellation, suspension, wakeup. Returns Option<Signal>.

title: wtf-worker: Implement process_workflow to continue execution
description: Signal workflow to continue after dequeue. Looks up workflow state, replays journal, executes pending commands. Updates workflow state after completion.

title: wtf-worker: Implement handle_timer_fired delivery
description: Deliver timer fired event to the target workflow. Timer contains invocation_id and timer_type. Workflow processes timer and may schedule more work.

title: wtf-worker: Implement deliver_signal to workflow
description: Deliver pending signal to target workflow. Signal types: Cancellation (workflow stops), Suspension (workflow pauses), Wakeup (workflow resumes).

title: wtf-worker: Implement main run loop with tokio::select
description: Main event loop using tokio::select! to multiplex between dequeue_workflow, check_expired_timers, and check_pending_signals. Enforces 3x parallelism limit from ADR-009.
